title: Mount containers in multi-stage build
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

Is it possible to mount another container in one container when multi-stage build?

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

I want to compile my software in a standard `ubi9/ubi` and run it in `ubi9/ubi-micro`.

To do this, I need to install the result to `ubi9/ubi-micro` by `make install` after compilation, and use the `yum` in `ubi9/ubi` to install dependencies for `ubi9/ubi-micro`.

So, is it possible to mount `ubi9/ubi-micro` in `ubi9/ubi`?

**Here is my build script:**
```bash
builder=$(buildah from registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi)
container=$(buildah from registry.access.redhat.com/ubi9/ubi-micro)
mountpoint=$(buildah mount ${container})

buildah run ${builder} -- yum install -y \
    clang-devel openssl-devel make cmake mariadb-connector-c-devel
buildah conifg --workingdir /opt/source ${builder}
buildah copy ${builder} . /opt/source

buildah run ${builder} -- touch ${mountpoint}

# Build and install to the runtime container
buildah run ${builder} -- bash -C <<EOF
    cmake /opt/source \
        -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release \
        -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER="clang++" \
        -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=${mountpoint}
    make -C /opt/source -j$(nproc)
    make install
EOF

# Install dependencies into the runtime container
buildah run ${builder} -- bash -C <<EOF
    yum install -y \
        --installroot ${mountpoint} --releasever 9 \
        --setopt install_weak_deps=false \
        --nodocs \
        libstdc++ mariadb-connector-c
    yum clean all --installroot ${mountpoint}
EOF

buildah commit --format docker ${container} runtime
buildah unmount ${container}
```

**Describe the results you received:**

Buildah can't find the mount point of `ubi9/ubi-micro` in `ubi9/ubi`.

```txt
touch: cannot touch '/home/mogeko/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/53073988766b618ed0fa1fd22709a731125ec2e6a484bb0ff4fc78a5af3ccb7f/merged': No such file or directory
error while running runtime: exit status 1
```

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
Listing... Done
buildah/jammy,now 1.23.1+ds1-2 arm64 [installed]
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.23.1
Go Version:      go1.17
Image Spec:      1.0.1
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:
image Version:   5.16.0
Git Commit:
Built:           Thu Jan  1 00:00:00 1970
OS/Arch:         linux/arm64
BuildPlatform:   linux/arm64/v8
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=22.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=jammy
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 22.04.2 LTS"
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 22.04.2 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION_ID="22.04"
VERSION="22.04.2 LTS (Jammy Jellyfish)"
VERSION_CODENAME=jammy
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
UBUNTU_CODENAME=jammy
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux mogeko 5.15.0-67-generic #74-Ubuntu SMP Wed Feb 22 14:14:39 UTC 2023 aarch64 aarch64 aarch64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
cat: /etc/containers/storage.conf: No such file or directory
```

comments.body:
Hey @mogeko ,

Please use `--mount` or `--volume`

Like
```console
buildah run --volume $mountpoint:/hello:z ${builder} -- ls /hello
```

Thanks I am closing this issue please re-open if you want :)
